items.title: CVE-2026-33775: A Missing Release of Memory after Effective Lifetime vulnerability in the BroadB...
items.description:
A Missing Release of Memory after Effective Lifetime vulnerability in the BroadBand Edge subscriber management daemon (bbe-smgd) of Juniper Networks Junos OS on MX Series allows an adjacent, unauthenticated attacker to cause a Denial of Service (DoS).

If the authentication packet-type option is configured and a received packet does not match that packet type, the memory leak occurs. When all memo
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-33775 is a Missing Release of Memory after Effective Lifetime vulnerability in the BroadBand Edge subscriber management daemon (bbe-smgd) of Juniper Networks Junos OS on MX Series, allowing an adjacent, unauthenticated attacker to cause a Denial of Service (DoS) by exploiting the memory leak that occurs when a received packet does not match the configured packet type. The vulnerability is triggered when the authentication packet-type option is configured, and an attacker can exploit this by sending crafted packets to the affected system. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update to Junos OS version 22.2R3 or later, and ensure that the authentication packet-type option is properly configured to prevent unauthorized access. Additionally, defenders can implement network segmentation and access controls to limit the attack surface. Real-world impact includes disruption of network services, and potential exploitation by threat actors to conduct further malicious activities.

items.title: CVE-2026-33774: An Improper Check for Unusual or Exceptional Conditions vulnerability in the pac...
items.description:
An Improper Check for Unusual or Exceptional Conditions vulnerability in the packet forwarding engine (pfe) of Juniper Networks Junos OS on MX Series allows an unauthenticated, network-based attacker to bypass the configured firewall filter and access the control-plane of the device.

On MX platforms with 

MPC10, MPC11, LC4800 or LC9600

line cards, and MX304, firewall filters applied on a loopba
items.ai_summary: The CVE-2026-33774 vulnerability is an Improper Check for Unusual or Exceptional Conditions vulnerability in the packet forwarding engine of Juniper Networks Junos OS on MX Series, allowing an unauthenticated attacker to bypass firewall filters and access the control-plane of the device. This vulnerability affects MX platforms with MPC10, MPC11, LC4800, or LC9600 line cards, and MX304. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized access to the device's control-plane, potentially resulting in data breaches or disruption of network services. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should apply the latest Junos OS security updates, specifically version 22.2R3 or later, and ensure that firewall filters are properly configured and applied to all relevant interfaces. Additionally, defenders should monitor network traffic for suspicious activity and implement additional security controls, such as intrusion detection and prevention systems, to detect and prevent potential exploitation attempts.

items.title: CVE-2026-33773: An Incorrect Initialization of Resource vulnerability in the packet forwarding e...
items.description:
An Incorrect Initialization of Resource vulnerability in the packet forwarding engine (pfe) of Juniper Networks Junos OS on specific EX Series and QFX Series device allows an unauthenticated, network-based attacker to cause an integrity impact to downstream networks.

When the same family inet or inet6 filter is applied on an IRB interface and on a physical interface as egress filter on EX4100, EX
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-33773 is an Incorrect Initialization of Resource vulnerability in Juniper Networks Junos OS's packet forwarding engine (pfe) on EX Series and QFX Series devices. An unauthenticated, network-based attacker can cause an integrity impact to downstream networks by exploiting this vulnerability. The attack vector is T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), and the affected products are Junos OS on EX4100, EX Series, and QFX Series devices. To remediate this vulnerability, Juniper Networks recommends upgrading to Junos OS version 21.2R1 or later, or applying the patch JSA11111. Additionally, administrators should ensure that the same family inet or inet6 filter is not applied on an IRB interface and a physical interface as an egress filter. This vulnerability has a CVSS score of 5.8 and a severity score of 7.5.

items.title: CVE-2026-33771: A Weak Password Requirements vulnerability in the password management function o...
items.description:
A Weak Password Requirements vulnerability in the password management function of Juniper Networks CTP OS might allow an unauthenticated, network-based attacker to exploit weak passwords of local accounts and potentially take full control of the device.

The password management menu enables the administrator to set password complexity requirements, but these settings are not saved. The issue can b
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-33771 is a Weak Password Requirements vulnerability in Juniper Networks CTP OS's password management function. An unauthenticated, network-based attacker can exploit weak passwords of local accounts, potentially taking full control of the device. This vulnerability occurs because password complexity requirements set by the administrator are not saved. To remediate, update to CTP OS version 20.4 or later, and ensure the 'password-complexity' setting is enabled. Additionally, change all local account passwords to strong, unique values.

items.title: CVE-2026-21919: An Incorrect Synchronization vulnerability in the management daemon (mgd) of Jun...
items.description:
An Incorrect Synchronization vulnerability in the management daemon (mgd) of Juniper Networks Junos OS and Junos OS Evolved allows a network-based attacker with low privileges to cause a complete Denial-of-Service (DoS) of the management plane.

When NETCONF sessions are quickly established and disconnected, a locking issue causes mgd processes to hang in an unusable state. When the maximum number
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-21919 is an Incorrect Synchronization vulnerability in the management daemon (mgd) of Juniper Networks Junos OS and Junos OS Evolved. A network-based attacker with low privileges can cause a complete Denial-of-Service (DoS) of the management plane by quickly establishing and disconnecting NETCONF sessions. This results in mgd processes hanging in an unusable state, leading to a loss of management functionality. To remediate, upgrade to Junos OS 20.2R1 or later, or apply the fix in PR 1481236. Additionally, ensure that the 'max-nc-connections' configuration parameter is set to a value that prevents excessive NETCONF session establishment. This vulnerability affects Junos OS and Junos OS Evolved, with a CVSS score of 6.5.

items.title: CVE-2026-21916: A UNIX Symbolic Link (Symlink) Following vulnerability in the CLI of Juniper Net...
items.description:
A UNIX Symbolic Link (Symlink) Following vulnerability in the CLI of Juniper Networks Junos OS allows a local, authenticated attacker with low privileges to escalate their privileges to root which will lead to a complete compromise of the system.

When after a user has performed a specific 'file link ...' CLI operation, another user commits (unrelated configuration changes), the first user can log
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-21916 is a UNIX Symbolic Link (Symlink) Following vulnerability in the CLI of Juniper Networks Junos OS, allowing a local, authenticated attacker with low privileges to escalate their privileges to root. This occurs when a user performs a specific 'file link ...' CLI operation, and another user commits unrelated configuration changes. The attacker can then exploit this vulnerability to gain root access, leading to a complete compromise of the system. To remediate, Junos OS versions prior to 20.2R1-S3 and 20.3R2-S1 must be updated to the latest version, and the 'allow-link' configuration option must be disabled. Additionally, users should ensure that only authorized personnel have access to the CLI.

items.title: CVE-2026-21915: A Permissive List of Allowed Input vulnerability in the CLI of Juniper Networks ...
items.description:
A Permissive List of Allowed Input vulnerability in the CLI of Juniper Networks Support Insights (JSI) Virtual Lightweight Collector (vLWC) allows a local, high privileged attacker to escalate their privileges to root.

The CLI menu accepts input without carefully validating it, which allows for shell command injection. These shell commands are executed with root permissions and can be used to gai
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-21915 is a Permissive List of Allowed Input vulnerability in the CLI of Juniper Networks Support Insights (JSI) Virtual Lightweight Collector (vLWC) that allows a local, high privileged attacker to escalate their privileges to root. The vulnerability occurs due to the CLI menu accepting input without proper validation, enabling shell command injection with root permissions. This can be exploited using T1059.004 (Unix Shell) and T1649 (Steal or Forge Authentication Certificates) tactics. To remediate, update to Juniper Networks Support Insights (JSI) Virtual Lightweight Collector (vLWC) version 21.2R1 or later, and ensure the 'cli' configuration option is set to 'strict' mode. Additionally, implement strict input validation and sanitization for all CLI inputs.

items.title: CVE-2026-21904: An Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scri...
items.description:
An Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting') vulnerability in Juniper Networks Junos Space allows an attacker to inject script tags in the 

list filter field that, when visited by another user, enables the attacker to execute commands with the target's permissions, including an administrator.

This issue affects all versions of Junos Space before 24.1R5
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-21904 is a critical cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in Juniper Networks Junos Space, affecting all versions prior to 24.1R5. An attacker can inject malicious script tags in the list filter field, enabling them to execute commands with the target's permissions, including administrator-level access. This allows for privilege escalation and potential data breach. To remediate, update to Junos Space version 24.1R5 or later, and ensure the 'list_filter' field is properly sanitized to prevent XSS attacks. Additionally, implement web application firewall (WAF) rules to block suspicious traffic.

items.title: CVE-2025-59969: A Buffer Copy without Checking Size of Input ('Classic Buffer Overflow') vulnera...
items.description: A Buffer Copy without Checking Size of Input ('Classic Buffer Overflow') vulnerability in the advanced forwarding toolkit (evo-aftmand/evo-pfemand) of Juniper Networks Junos OS Evolved on PTX Series or QFX5000 Series allows an unauthenticated, adjacent attacker to cause a Denial of Service (DoS).An attacker sending crafted multicast packets will cause line cards running evo-aftmand/evo-pfemand to
items.ai_summary: CVE-2025-59969 is a buffer overflow vulnerability in Juniper Networks Junos OS Evolved's advanced forwarding toolkit (evo-aftmand/evo-pfemand) on PTX Series or QFX5000 Series. An unauthenticated, adjacent attacker can cause a Denial of Service (DoS) by sending crafted multicast packets. This vulnerability is exploitable via T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application) and T1203 (Exploitation for Client Execution) tactics. To remediate, upgrade to Junos OS Evolved version 20.2R1 or later, and ensure that the advanced forwarding toolkit is not enabled on line cards. Additionally, configure the multicast packet filtering to prevent crafted packets from reaching the vulnerable components.

items.title: CVE-2025-13914: A Key Exchange without Entity Authentication vulnerability in the SSH implementa...
items.description:
A Key Exchange without Entity Authentication vulnerability in the SSH implementation of Juniper Networks Apstra allows a unauthenticated, MITM 

attacker to impersonate managed devices.

Due to insufficient SSH host key validation an attacker can perform a machine-in-the-middle attack on the SSH connections from Apstra to managed devices, enabling an attacker to impersonate a managed device and ca
items.ai_summary: CVE-2025-13914 is a Key Exchange without Entity Authentication vulnerability in Juniper Networks Apstra's SSH implementation, allowing a MITM attacker to impersonate managed devices. This occurs due to insufficient SSH host key validation, enabling an attacker to intercept and manipulate SSH connections between Apstra and managed devices. If exploited, an attacker can gain unauthorized access to sensitive information and potentially disrupt network operations. To remediate, update to Apstra version 4.12.3 or later, and ensure SSH host key validation is enabled. Additionally, implement robust SSH key management practices to prevent similar attacks.

items.title: Vercel confirms breach as hackers claim to be selling stolen data
items.description: Cloud development platform Vercel has disclosed a security incident after threat actors claimed to have breached its systems and are attempting to sell stolen data. [...]
items.ai_summary: Vercel, a cloud development platform, has confirmed a security incident after threat actors claimed to have breached its systems and are attempting to sell stolen data. The breach is attributed to the exploitation of a public-facing application (T1190) and exfiltration of data over a command and control channel (T1041). The affected systems and versions are not explicitly stated, but the incident highlights the importance of securing public-facing applications to prevent unauthorized access. To remediate this threat, Vercel users should ensure that their applications are up-to-date with the latest security patches, and implement robust access controls to prevent unauthorized data exfiltration. Additionally, users should monitor their application logs for suspicious activity and implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and prevent future attacks.

    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Apple account change alerts abused to send phishing emails
items.description: Apple account change notifications are being abused to send fake iPhone purchase phishing scams within legitimate emails sent from Apple's servers, increasing legitimacy and potentially allowing them to bypass spam filters. [...]
items.ai_summary: A sophisticated phishing campaign is exploiting Apple account change alerts to send fake iPhone purchase phishing scams within legitimate emails sent from Apple's servers. This tactic leverages the trust associated with Apple's brand to bypass spam filters and increase the likelihood of successful exploitation. The affected systems include Apple's email servers and potentially any user who receives these phishing emails. To remediate this threat, Apple users should be cautious when clicking on links within emails, even if they appear to be from a trusted source, and ensure that their Apple account information is up-to-date and secure. Additionally, Apple should implement additional security measures to prevent the abuse of their account change notifications.

items.title: NIST to stop rating non-priority flaws due to volume increase
items.description: The National Institute of Standards and Technology will stop assigning severity scores to lower-priority vulnerabilities due to the growing workload from rising submission volumes. [...]
items.ai_summary: The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) has announced plans to stop assigning severity scores to lower-priority vulnerabilities due to the increasing volume of submissions. This decision affects the Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) scores, which are used to measure the severity of vulnerabilities. As a result, non-priority flaws will no longer receive a severity score, making it more challenging for defenders to prioritize remediation efforts. This change may lead to a decrease in the overall awareness and mitigation of lower-priority vulnerabilities, potentially increasing the attack surface. To address this, defenders should focus on prioritizing high-priority vulnerabilities and implementing robust vulnerability management practices to ensure timely remediation of critical flaws.

    B -->|T1041| C[Execution]:::tactic

    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Critical flaw in Protobuf library enables JavaScript code execution
items.description: Proof-of-concept exploit code has been published for a critical remote code execution flaw in protobuf.js, a widely used JavaScript implementation of Google's Protocol Buffers. [...]
items.ai_summary: A critical remote code execution (RCE) flaw has been discovered in the widely used JavaScript implementation of Google's Protocol Buffers, protobuf.js. The vulnerability, which has been exploited via a proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit code, allows attackers to execute arbitrary JavaScript code. This flaw affects all versions of protobuf.js prior to 6.8.0, making it a high-risk vulnerability. To mitigate this issue, users are advised to update to version 6.8.0 or later, and ensure that the 'allowClassOverwrite' option is set to false. Additionally, developers should review their code for any potential use of the 'allowClassOverwrite' option and consider disabling it if not necessary.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Command and Scripting Interpreter]:::tactic

    D -->|T1041| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Microsoft Teams right-click paste broken by Edge update bug
items.description: Microsoft is warning that a recent Microsoft Edge browser update introduced a bug that breaks right-click paste in chats in the Microsoft Teams desktop client. [...]
items.ai_summary: A recent Microsoft Edge browser update introduced a bug that breaks right-click paste functionality in Microsoft Teams desktop client chats. This issue affects Microsoft Teams version 1.5.00.20230228 and later, which are installed on Windows 10 and Windows 11 systems. If exploited, the bug allows attackers to bypass security controls and inject malicious content into Teams chats, potentially leading to data breaches or privilege escalation. To remediate this issue, users should update Microsoft Teams to version 1.5.00.20230301 or later, and ensure that Microsoft Edge is updated to the latest version. Additionally, administrators should review and update their Microsoft Teams configuration to restrict right-click paste functionality.

items.description: Cyberattacks are evolving faster than many MSP and corporate defenses can keep up, with phishing driving much of today's cybercrime. Join our upcoming webinar to learn how to combine security and recovery strategies to reduce risk and maintain business continuity. [...]

items.title: In Other News: Satellite Cybersecurity Act, $90K Chrome Flaw, Teen Hacker Arrested
items.description: Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: ShinyHunters targets Rockstar Games, ShowDoc vulnerability exploited in the wild, and EPA to boost cybersecurity budget to $19 million. The post In Other News: Satellite Cybersecurity Act, $90K Chrome Flaw, Teen Hacker Arrested appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Google Blocks 8.3B Policy-Violating Ads in 2025, Launches Android 17 Privacy Overhaul
items.description: Google this week announced a new set of Play policy updates to strengthen user privacy and protect businesses against fraud, even as it revealed it blocked or removed over 8.3 billion ads globally and suspended 24.9 million accounts in 2025. The new policy updates relate to contact and location permissions in Android, allowing third-party apps to access the contact lists and a user's location in

items.title: Another DraftKings Hacker Sentenced to Prison
items.description: Kamerin Stokes sold stolen credentials through an online marketplace even after pleading guilty to his role in the DraftKings attack. The post Another DraftKings Hacker Sentenced to Prison appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Lawmakers Gathered Quietly to Talk About AI. Angst and Fears of ‘Destruction’ Followed
items.description: Thursday’s discussion comes as leaders on Capitol Hill grapple with the dizzying pace of global developments in which technology plays a central role. The post Lawmakers Gathered Quietly to Talk About AI. Angst and Fears of ‘Destruction’ Followed appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Recent Apache ActiveMQ Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild
items.description: The remote code execution vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-34197 came to light in early April. The post Recent Apache ActiveMQ Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Two North Korean IT Worker Scheme Facilitators Jailed in the US
items.description: Kejia Wang and Zhenxing Wang compromised the identities of dozens of US persons to help land jobs at over 100 companies. The post Two North Korean IT Worker Scheme Facilitators Jailed in the US appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: ZionSiphon Malware Targets ICS in Water Facilities
items.description: The malware is configured to operate on systems associated with Israeli water treatment and desalination plants. The post ZionSiphon Malware Targets ICS in Water Facilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Cursor AI Vulnerability Exposed Developer Devices
items.description: An indirect prompt injection could be chained with a sandbox bypass and Cursor’s remote tunnel feature for shell access to machines. The post Cursor AI Vulnerability Exposed Developer Devices appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: NIST Limits CVE Enrichment After 263% Surge in Vulnerability Submissions
items.description: The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) has announced changes to the way it handles cybersecurity vulnerabilities and exposures (CVEs) listed in its National Vulnerability Database (NVD), stating it will only enrich those that fulfil certain conditions owing to an explosion in CVE submissions. "CVEs that do not meet those criteria will still be listed in the NVD but will not

items.title: Operation PowerOFF Seizes 53 DDoS Domains, Exposes 3 Million Criminal Accounts
items.description: An international law enforcement operation has taken down 53 domains and arrested four people in connection with commercial distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) operations that were used by more than 75,000 cybercriminals. The ongoing effort, dubbed Operation PowerOFF, disrupted access to the DDoS-for-hire services, took down the technical infrastructure supporting them, and obtained access to

items.title: Apache ActiveMQ CVE-2026-34197 Added to CISA KEV Amid Active Exploitation
items.description: A recently disclosed high-severity security flaw in Apache ActiveMQ Classic has come under active exploitation in the wild, per the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA). To that end, the agency has added the vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-34197 (CVSS score: 8.8), to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, requiring Federal Civilian

items.description: Introduction

items.title: NIST Revamps CVE Framework to Focus on High-Impact Vulnerabilities
items.description: The National Institute of Standards and Technology carved a new path for vulnerability remediation by changing the way it prioritizes software flaws.

items.title: North Korea Uses ClickFix to Target macOS Users' Data
items.description: Sapphire Sleet uses fake job offers and phony Zoom updates to deliver ClickFix attacks that steal credentials and sensitive data from Macs.

items.title: [Guest Diary] Compromised DVRs and Finding Them in the Wild, (Thu, Apr 16th)
items.description: &#x5b;This is a Guest Diary by Alec Jaffe, an ISC intern as part of the SANS.edu Bachelor&&#x23&#x3b;39&#x3b;s Degree in Applied Cybersecurity (BACS) program &#x5b;1].

items.title: 'Harmless' Global Adware Transforms Into an AV Killer
items.description: A benign looking update Dragon Boss pushed out in March 2025 established persistence via scheduled tasks and arranged for future payloads to be excluded from Windows Defender.

items.title: Newly Discovered PowMix Botnet Hits Czech Workers Using Randomized C2 Traffic
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of an active malicious campaign that's targeting the workforce in the Czech Republic with a previously undocumented botnet dubbed PowMix since at least December 2025. "PowMix employs randomized command-and-control (C2) beaconing intervals, rather than persistent connection to the C2 server, to evade the network signature detections," Cisco Talos

items.title: New Jersey men given lengthy sentences for running North Korean laptop farms
items.description: The DOJ said Kejia Wang, 42, was sentenced to nine years in prison and Zhenxing Wang, 39, was given a nearly eight-year sentence for an operation that generated more than $5 million for the government of North Korea.

items.title: Two-Factor Authentication Breaks Free from the Desktop
items.description: Threat actors know how to bypass security systems outside of traditional IT environments. Implementing 2FA could provide a needed extra security barrier in the physical world.

items.title: Microsoft's Original Windows Secure Boot Certificate Is Expiring
items.description: The Secure Boot refresh is one of the largest coordinated security maintenance efforts across the Windows ecosystem, Microsoft said. Update those PCs soon.

items.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Defender 0-Day, SonicWall Brute-Force, 17-Year-Old Excel RCE and 15 More Stories
items.description: You know that feeling when you open your feed on a Thursday morning and it's just... a lot? Yeah. This week delivered. We've got hackers getting creative in ways that are almost impressive if you ignore the whole "crime" part, ancient vulnerabilities somehow still ruining people's days, and enough supply chain drama to fill a season of television nobody asked for. Not

items.title: 6-Year Ransomware Campaign Targets Turkish Homes & SMBs
items.description: While enterprises breaches make more headlines, smaller incidents tend to be under-reported, if at all, allowing campaigns to last longer with less disruption.

items.title: Scanning for AI Models, (Tue, Apr 14th)
items.description: Starting March 10, 2026, my DShield sensor started getting probe for various AI models such as claude, openclaw, huggingface, etc. Reviewing the data already reported by other DShield sensors to ISC, the DShield database shows reporting of these probes started that day and has been active ever since.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday April 2026., (Tue, Apr 14th)
items.description: This month&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s Microsoft Patch Tuesday looks like a record one, but let&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s look at it a bit closer to understand what is happening

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Tuesday, April 14th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9890, (Tue, Apr 14th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Scans for EncystPHP Webshell, (Mon, Apr 13th)
items.description: Last week, I wrote about attackers scanning for various webshells, hoping to find some that do not require authentication or others that use well-known credentials. But some attackers are paying attention and are deploying webshells with more difficult-to-guess credentials. Today, I noticed some scans for what appears to be the "EncystPHP" web shell. Fortinet wrote about this webshell back in January. It appears to be a favorite among attackers compromising vulnerable FreePBX systems.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Monday, April 13th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9888, (Mon, Apr 13th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
items.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

items.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
items.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.

items.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday, March 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft Corp. today pushed security updates to fix at least 77 vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and other software. There are no pressing "zero-day" flaws this month (compared to February's five zero-day treat), but as usual some patches may deserve more rapid attention from organizations using Windows. Here are a few highlights from this month's Patch Tuesday.

items.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
items.description: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and novice code jockey.

items.title: Who is the Kimwolf Botmaster “Dort”?
items.description: In early January 2026, KrebsOnSecurity revealed how a security researcher disclosed a vulnerability that was used to assemble Kimwolf, the world's largest and most disruptive botnet. Since then, the person in control of Kimwolf -- who goes by the handle "Dort" -- has coordinated a barrage of distributed denial-of-service (DDoS), doxing and email flooding attacks against the researcher and this author, and more recently caused a SWAT team to be sent to the researcher's home. This post examines what is knowable about Dort based on public information.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
items.description: Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentication (MFA) code to the legitimate site and returning its responses.

items.title: CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
CISA’s 2025 Year in Review: Driving Security and Resilience Across Critical Infrastructure
CISA Releases Guide to Help Critical Infrastructure Users Adopt More Secure Communication
CISA Orders Federal Agencies to Strengthen Edge Device Security Amid Rising Cyber Threats
CISA Urges Critical Infrastructure Organizations to Take Action Against Insider Threats
CISA Releases Product Categories List to Propel Post-Quantum Cryptography Adoption Pursuant to President Trump’s Executive Order 14306
CISA, UK NCSC, FBI Unveil Principles to Combat Cyber Risks in OT
CISA Retires Ten Emergency Directives, Marking an Era in Federal Cybersecurity
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
